task_completion.description: Add the rivers shapefile to the gvSIG view
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Table of Contents (TOC) on the left shows 'ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines' added to the view, and river lines are rendered on the map.

task_completion.description: Select Brazil from the countries layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The polygon representing Brazil in the 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' layer is highlighted (usually in yellow or a distinct selection color) on the map view.
A geoprocessing or clip tool dialog window is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Configure the Clip tool with the correct layers and output path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Clip tool dialog shows the input layer as the rivers layer, the clip layer as the countries layer (with an indication that only selected features are used), and the output file path set to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/brazil_rivers.shp'.

task_completion.description: Execute the clip operation to create the new shapefile
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new layer (e.g., 'brazil_rivers') appears in the TOC, and/or the map shows the clipped river lines strictly within the boundary of Brazil. Alternatively, a file manager shows the newly created 'brazil_rivers.shp' in the exports folder.

integrity.description: The agent used the gvSIG GUI to perform the clip operation rather than terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with gvSIG menus, toolbars, and geoprocessing dialogs, not a terminal window executing GDAL/ogr2ogr commands.

integrity.description: The agent actually loaded and interacted with the shapefiles in gvSIG
integrity.visual_evidence: The gvSIG application window is visible with the map view rendering the actual shapefile geometries.

integrity.description: The agent entered the exact requested output path
integrity.visual_evidence: The text '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/brazil_rivers.shp' is visible in the output file path field of the geoprocessing dialog or save prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified paths: Output must be saved to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/brazil_rivers.shp'. Rivers source is '/home/ga/gvsig_data/rivers/ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines.shp'. Countries source is '/home/ga/gvsig_data/countries/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp'. Note on visual verification: The 110m rivers dataset contains very few global features. Clipping it to Brazil will result in exactly 2 river features visible within the Brazil boundary (approximate bounding box center X is -60.22). Do not expect a dense network of rivers.
